title: Tempus Fugit.
content:
Lovely Spring,
    A brief sweet thing,
    Is swift on the wing;
    Gracious Summer,
    A slow sweet comer,
    Hastens past;
    Autumn while sweet
    Is all incomplete
    With a moaning blast, -
    Nothing can last,
    Can be cleaved unto,
    Can be dwelt upon;
    It is hurried through,
    It is come and gone,
    Undone it cannot be done,
    It is ever to do,
    Ever old, ever new,
    Ever waxing old
    And lapsing to Winter cold.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Christina Georgina Rossetti delivers a powerful performance in "Tempus Fugit."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
